name: Anonymized Dev-Metrics
summary: A platform-owned, privacy-first sibling vocabulary over the analytics contract (#1849/#1797). Reuses the transport-agnostic, void fire-and-forget, swappable-sink-via-DI shape of the Custom Tracker, but narrows it to a CLOSED dev-action event (command · exitCode · version · os · arch · ci · timestamp) so the channel can never carry PII. Anonymized by a salted rotating-daily install-id — per-day distinct-developer dedupe with no persistent cross-day fingerprint (fixed, not configurable). Opt-IN by default (first-run prompt + one env-var/flag escape); no data flows until the developer affirmatively consents. The sink is a DEDICATED PLATFORM tracker, never a product-analytics vendor. Reference emitter lives in Frontier UI (plugs/webanalytics/devMetrics.ts); the hosted aggregation endpoint and the enterprise-policy precedence layer are decoupled to separate items.
description:
Design Philosophy

To decide what to build next, the platform needs anonymized counts of developer actions (installs, builds, tests) — never per-developer identifiable data. This channel rides the same contract shape as product analytics (a swappable track()-style sink resolved via DI) but is a distinct, platform-owned stream: a dev-metrics stream can only route to a dedicated platform sink, never a consumer's Segment / GA4 / Mixpanel.

Closed vocabulary (the What)

The command is drawn from a fixed set — install · build · test · dev · lint · check · generate · publish — so the aggregate cannot leak a project-specific command name. Every other field is a low-cardinality, non-identifying dimension.

Interface Protocol

type DevCommand = 'install' | 'build' | 'test' | 'dev' | 'lint' | 'check' | 'generate' | 'publish';

interface DevMetricEvent {
    command: DevCommand;
    exitCode: number;
    version: string;   // CLI / platform version
    os: string;        // 'darwin' | 'linux' | 'win32'
    arch: string;      // 'arm64' | 'x64'
    ci: boolean;       // bare flag, never the CI vendor
    timestamp: string; // ISO-8601
    installId: string; // salted rotating-daily id (per-day distinct, no persistent fingerprint)
}

interface DevMetricsSink {
    key: string;
    record(event: DevMetricEvent): void; // fire-and-forget, never a gate
}

Privacy rulings (#1797)

  • Salted rotating-daily install-id — dedupes distinct developers within a day, rotates so it is not a persistent fingerprint. Fixed, not a knob.
  • Opt-IN — consent defaults to do not emit; a first-run prompt plus a one-flag escape drive the grant.
